name: wheat-dry-single-sphere
material: wheat grain, dry, cohesionless (literature ground truth; source experiments used AK58 at ~14% moisture)

engine.build: lmp_auto from source, arm64 macOS, Open MPI, Boost, USE_VTK=OFF (see ROADMAP Phase 0); responses verified on this build — re-verify a few seeds after any rebuild

contact_model.pair_style: gran model hertz tangential history rolling_friction epsd2
contact_model.cohesion: none (dry wheat is cohesionless at storage moisture)
contact_model.particle_shape: sphere (mu_r absorbs grain-shape effects — the locked Phase-3 modelling decision)

fric.description: particle-particle sliding friction; one member of the equivalence family — see equivalence_family for the stated uncertainty

rollfric.description: particle-particle rolling friction (epsd2); anti-correlated with fric along the family

rest.description: restitution — negligible influence on the calibrated quasi-static/rolling responses (Phase-7 sensitivity: delta 0.02); effectively free within 0.3-0.7

equivalence_family.constraint: fric 0.25-0.60 anti-correlated with rollfric 0.22-0.12 (rest free): every anchor matches BOTH calibrated responses within spread — a single parameter point is NOT identified by the calibrated responses
equivalence_family.caveat: the family is breakable only by a measured orifice flow rate (drawdown discriminates 8x seed noise along the family — Phase-9 probe — but no literature target is tight enough; a physical measurement at +/-2 g/s would localize a unique point)

fixed_inputs.youngs_modulus_note: softened 2-3 orders below reality for timestep (standard DEM practice; AoR verified insensitive 1e7-5e7); pair with timestep_s

wall_acrylic.source: Sugirbay et al. 2022 Table 11 (wheat-acrylic, calibrated on their 7-sphere clumps — carried as a fixed protocol input)

aor.protocol: static AoR, lifted cylinder R 0.040 m / H 0.100 m, 4000 spheres, lift 10 mm/s (method-bound; templates/aor.in)

drum.protocol: dynamic AoR, vertical rotating drum diam. 150 mm x 25 mm, 5 rpm, 50% fill, co-rotating acrylic covers (templates/drum.in; axial length halved vs the published 50 mm — documented deviation)

bulk_density.note: falls out of the fixed PSD + particle density with zero calibration — a free consistency check

validation.scenario: drum45 hold-out: acrylic drum inclined 45 deg, 5 rpm, 50% fill (Sugirbay et al. 2022, never used in calibration)
validation.criterion: |predicted - 43.65| <= 2.0*sigma_45 = 5.84 deg on the representative set, with n_ok >= 4 of 5 seeds and no steadiness-drift warnings
deviations: axial length 25 mm vs published 50 mm — biases the 45-deg comparison MORE than vertical (bed rests on the cover, trace measured at the cover); bounded by a one-off 50 mm sensitivity run in M3
single spheres vs 7-sphere clumps — mu_r absorbs shape effects (locked Phase-3 decision)
2-5 seeds vs 5 physical reps x 4 frames

evidence.optimizer_best: none — the Phase-9 multi-response optimizer study was cancelled at the M4 gate (drum degenerate with heap); results/phase9-drum/best.json is a tombstone for a test artifact (invalidated 2026-06-13)

reproduction.note: tolerances ~= the seed-noise floor: exact on this machine's cache; absorbs compiler/MPI nondeterminism on a fresh build

valid_for: quasi-static and slow-flow bulk behaviour (heaps, slow drums, hopper drawdown at moderate rates)
wheat-scale spheres at the stated PSD; geometries at the calibration scale (10^3-10^5 particles)
not_calibrated_for: cohesive or moist material (no cohesion model)
rate-sensitive responses far from the calibrated protocols (the heap test is strongly lift-speed sensitive)
